data.title: audit: normalize shared skills to current SKILL.md structure
data.description:
## Problem

The moved `skills/` set does not consistently match the structure taught by the repo's own skill-authoring guidance.

Concrete examples from the current tree:

* `skills/skogai-argc/SKILL.md` is about 677 lines
* `skills/skogai-bats-testing/SKILL.md` is about 677 lines
* `skills/skogai-docs/SKILL.md` is about 523 lines
* `skills/skogai-wrapup/SKILL.md` uses `name: wrapup` while the directory is `skogai-wrapup`
* many moved skills still use markdown-heading bodies even where `skogai-skills`/`skogai-routing` teach XML- and router-oriented structure

## Scope

* audit repo-local skills for frontmatter, naming, description quality, and body structure
* decide where a skill should remain simple vs be split into workflows/references/templates
* split oversized single-file skills only where that actually improves loading and reviewability
* fix directory/name mismatches and weak or incomplete trigger descriptions
* explicitly review risky auto-actions while normalizing semantics, especially `skogai-wrapup`

## Done when

* every moved skill has intentionally chosen structure rather than inherited drift
* obvious frontmatter/name mismatches are fixed
* oversized skills have either been split or explicitly justified as single-file
* descriptions consistently explain both what the skill does and when it should trigger
* review notes identify any remaining exceptions instead of leaving them implicit
